title: 10 best herbal capsules for benign prostatic hypertrophy
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for benign prostatic hypertrophy are natural supplements that contain a blend of herbs and botanicals specifically designed to alleviate symptoms associated with enlarged prostate, also known as benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH). These capsules work by reducing inflammation, improving urine flow, and relaxing the muscles in the prostate gland. Examples include Saw Palmetto, Pygeum, and Stinging Nettle, which have been shown to improve urinary function, reduce frequency of urination, and alleviate symptoms such as nighttime awakenings. By taking these herbal capsules, men with BPH can experience improved overall well-being, increased confidence, and a better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to the growth and enlargement of the prostate gland. By blocking this conversion, saw palmetto may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with BPH, such as frequent urination, urgency, and weak stream. Additionally, saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce prostatic swelling and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Inhibition of 5-alpha-reductase: Saw palmetto's active compounds, such as fatty acids and phytosterols, inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH growth and symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Saw palmetto's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the prostate gland, which can alleviate symptoms of BPH such as enlarged prostate, frequent urination, and nocturia.
Anti-proliferative effects: The herb's bioactive compounds may slow down or stop the growth of abnormal prostate cells, reducing the risk of BPH progression and related complications.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Saw palmetto seeds are a rich source of fatty acids, particularly oleic acid and linoleic acid, which have been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH.
Fruits: The berries or fruits of the saw palmetto plant contain beta-sitosterol, a compound that has been found to reduce inflammation and improve urinary symptoms in men with BPH.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried serenoa repens leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for processing.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Sift the powdered herb through a fine-mesh sieve to ensure uniform particle size and remove any lumps.
Mix 200-300mg of the sifted serenoa repens powder with 100-150mg of potato starch or magnesium stearate as excipients in a small bowl.
Fill #0 capsules or gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal tightly to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain bioactive compounds that have a beneficial impact on prostate health. The herb's ability to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle tissue can help alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination and difficulty starting or stopping the flow of urine. Additionally, fenugreek has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to benign prostatic hypertrophy. By addressing these underlying issues, fenugreek capsules may provide natural relief from BPH symptoms.
remedy_properties: Fiber: Fenugreek's high fiber content helps reduce prostate inflammation and relieve symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and nocturia by increasing stool bulk and reducing pressure on the prostate gland.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in fenugreek capsules, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage prostate cells and contribute to BPH progression, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

5-alpha-reductase inhibitors: Fenugreek contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase, which converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT). Elevated DHT levels are thought to contribute to BPH development, so reducing its production can help alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fenugreek plant are rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) by reducing inflammation and improving urinary function.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain a high amount of mucilage, a gel-like substance that can help soothe and calm the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms such as frequency and urgency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried fenugreek seeds from trusted sources and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried fenugreek seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground fenugreek powder with 250 grams of honey to create a uniform blend and set it aside for 1 hour.
Sift the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps and ensure a smooth consistency before filling capsules for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the sifting mixture and seal them tightly to store in an airtight container for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antiprogestative properties. The capsules help to reduce swelling in the prostate gland, which can alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination, weak stream, and dribbling. Additionally, stinging nettle's ability to relax the smooth muscle tissues surrounding the urethra helps to improve urine flow and reduce pressure on the bladder. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from BPH symptoms without compromising overall health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which can alleviate symptoms of BPH such as urinary frequency and discomfort.

5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Nettle extracts contain compounds that inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone linked to BPH. By blocking this conversion, nettle may help slow the progression of BPH.

Antioxidant and anti-oxidative stress properties: Nettle capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, which can contribute to cell damage and tissue degeneration associated with BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Seeds: The seeds contain a unique combination of fatty acids and other nutrients that may help reduce inflammation and improve urinary flow, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules for benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves from a trusted supplier or cultivate them organically in your garden.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6-8 hours or air-drying for several days.
Grind the dried stinging nettle leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 400mg of powdered stinging nettle with 200mg of vitamin e oil and 100mg of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain mucilages that soothe and calm the inflamed prostate tissue, reducing discomfort and urgency. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow help to shrink the enlarged prostate, alleviating symptoms such as frequent urination, weak stream, and difficulty starting or stopping the flow of urine. Additionally, marshmallow's ability to reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urethra helps to alleviate associated symptoms like burning sensation while urinating.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow capsules contain mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance that helps soothe and calm inflammation in the prostate gland, reducing symptoms such as urinary frequency and urgency.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in marshmallow capsules help neutralize free radicals that can damage prostatic tissue and contribute to BPH development and progression, thereby reducing oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Marshmallow capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the prostate gland, alleviating symptoms such as pelvic pain and urinary retention.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated tissues in the prostate gland.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis in quantities of 50 grams for processing.
Grind the dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of the ground marshmallow root powder with 30 grams of cornstarch and 10 grams of gelatin.
Add water to create a paste then fill into gelatin capsules using a capsule machine.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the prostate. This can help alleviate symptoms of BPH, such as urinary frequency and difficulty starting urination. Additionally, the beta-sitosterol present in silver birch may help to relax the smooth muscle in the prostate gland, further reducing compression on the urethra and alleviating symptoms of an enlarged prostate.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Silver birch capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin, which may help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland and alleviate symptoms of BPH.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in silver birch capsules may help protect the prostate cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to BPH development.

Alpha-blocker-like activity: Silver birch capsules have been shown to exhibit alpha-blocker-like activity, which may help relax the smooth muscle tissue in the prostate gland and urethra, improving urinary flow and reducing symptoms of BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland.
Barks: Contains xanthones and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cancer cells.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried betula pendula leaves and 20g of dried saw palmetto berries for a 10:1 ratio.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 400mg of the herb powder with 100mg of silica gel in a small bowl for 30 seconds.
Fill empty capsules with the herb-silica mixture, making sure to fill each capsule to the brim, taking about 10-15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use to allow for proper curing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Puncture vine capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain a natural compound called tripterine, which has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects on prostate cells. By reducing inflammation and slowing down cell growth, tripterine may help alleviate symptoms of BPH such as frequent urination, nocturia, and bladder outlet obstruction. Additionally, puncture vine capsules may also improve urinary flow and reduce the risk of complications associated with BPH, making them a promising natural alternative for managing this common condition.
remedy_properties: Cytotoxic compounds: Puncture vine capsules contain cytotoxic compounds like glycosides and alkaloids that have been shown to inhibit the growth and proliferation of prostate cells, helping to reduce the size of an enlarged prostate.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The herbal extract has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), such as pain, discomfort, and urinary frequency, by reducing inflammation in the prostate gland.

α-Blocker-like activity: Puncture vine capsules have been found to possess α-blocker-like activity, which means they can help relax smooth muscle fibers in the prostate and urinary tract, improving urine flow and reducing symptoms of BPH.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, such as beta-sitosterol and lupeol, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy by reducing inflammation and improving urinary flow.
Barks: Containing flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the prostate gland and alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality dried tribulus terrestris herb weighing 100 grams and store it in an airtight container for future use.
Grind the dried tribulus terrestris herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 30 seconds.
Mix 400 milligrams of the powdered tribulus terrestris with 200 milligrams of silica powder and store the blend in an airtight container at room temperature.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal blend and seal them tightly to prevent contamination or moisture exposure for about 1 hour.
Label and date the filled capsules accurately, and store them in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until consumed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tomato capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain a concentrated extract of lycopene, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland. Lycopene's ability to quench free radicals and mitigate oxidative damage can help alleviate symptoms associated with BPH, such as urinary frequency, urgency, and straining during urination. Additionally, lycopene may also inhibit the growth of prostatic cells, reducing the size of the enlarged prostate tissue.
remedy_properties: Saw Palmetto: Inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement.
Pygeum: Reduces inflammation and improves urinary flow by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which can constrict the prostate gland.
Beta-Sitosterol: May help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of BPH by decreasing the proliferation of prostate cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tomato roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Fruits: The fruits of the tomato plant contain lycopene, a carotenoid compound that has been shown to improve prostate health and reduce symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried solanum lycopersicum fruit from a trusted source for quality control.
Grind the dried fruit into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 500 milliliters of distilled water to create an infusion, steeping it at 80 degrees celsius for 45 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid for further processing within 1 hour.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal extract and seal them tightly to create the final product within 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because of its rich content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and terpenes. These compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination and painful ejaculation associated with BPH. The capsule's natural extracts also exhibit anti-proliferative properties, which may slow down the growth of enlarged prostate cells, thereby helping to alleviate urinary tract obstruction and other related issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maidenhair tree capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with BPH, making urination less uncomfortable.
Alpha-blocker activity: The capsules contain compounds that mimic the action of alpha-blockers, helping to relax the muscles in the prostate gland and improve urine flow.
Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair tree capsules are rich in antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and damage to the prostate cells, which can contribute to BPH development and progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair Tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing prostate inflammation associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair Tree are rich in ginkgolides, which have been found to relax smooth muscle tissue and reduce prostate constriction, thereby alleviating symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source weighing approximately 500 grams.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves in a temperature-controlled environment at 40°c for 48 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, yielding 300 grams of powdered extract.
Mix the powdered extract with 300 grams of tapioca starch and 100 grams of glycerin in a large bowl, ensuring uniform blending.
Fill softgel capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately, containing approximately 500mg of herbal ginkgo biloba per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain a compound called arbutin, which is converted to hydroquinone in the body. This natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent has been shown to reduce swelling and relieve symptoms associated with an enlarged prostate gland. By shrinking the prostate tissue, bearberry capsules may help alleviate urinary frequency, nocturia, and other common issues associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy, providing a natural alternative for men seeking relief from these bothersome symptoms.
remedy_properties: Arbutin: Helps to reduce urine production and decrease the risk of kidney stone formation by inhibiting the absorption of glucose in the kidneys, thereby reducing the concentration of minerals in the urine that can contribute to BPH symptoms.

Quercetin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as urinary frequency, urgency, and nocturia associated with BPH by reducing inflammation in the prostate gland.

Hydroquinone: Has been shown to have a specific affinity for binding to and inhibiting the action of 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH development.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Barks: The barks of the bearberry plant are rich in proanthocyanidins, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested material in a warm air circulation oven at 50°c for 3 hours or until crumbly.
Crush the dried arctostaphylos uva ursi into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 500mg of the powdered herb with 100mg of silica gel in a small bowl to prevent caking.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herb-silica mixture, sealing each capsule tightly to preserve potency for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with enlarged prostate. The herb's natural compounds, including berberine and hydrastis, have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that contribute to prostate enlargement. By reducing inflammation and prostaglandin levels, goldenseal capsules may help alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and incomplete emptying of the bladder, offering relief for men with BPH.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells, reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, and improve symptoms of BPH, likely due to its ability to target specific cellular pathways involved in prostate cell proliferation.

Isothiocyanates: Goldenseal's isothiocyanates have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of BPH such as frequent urination and nocturia by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress within the prostate gland.

Caffeoylglucosides: These bioactive compounds in goldenseal have been demonstrated to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to their ability to improve urinary flow and reduce symptoms of BPH by targeting underlying infections or inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain a high concentration of berberine, a bioactive compound that has been shown to help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH) by reducing inflammation and improving urinary flow.

Rhizomes: Goldenseal rhizomes are rich in berberine and other alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating the inflammatory and bacterial components of BPH.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and grind 400 milligrams of dried goldenseal root powder into a fine consistency.
Measure and combine one tablespoon of gelatin with one cup of boiling water to create a gel-like substance.
Mix the ground hydrastis canadensis with the gel-like substance for 30 minutes to ensure uniform distribution.
Pour the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal according to manufacturer's instructions.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat benign prostatic hypertrophy with herbal capsules is to choose a formula that contains Saw Palmetto, Pygeum, and Stinging Nettle. These herbs have been shown to help reduce symptoms of BPH by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which contributes to prostate enlargement. Take the recommended dose daily for at least 6 weeks to see noticeable improvements in urinary flow and frequency.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to benign prostatic hypertrophy that are treated with herbal capsules are urinary tract issues such as frequent urination, incontinence, and prostate enlargement. Herbal remedies like saw palmetto, Pygeum africanum, and beta-sitosterol have been used to alleviate symptoms of enlarged prostate, as well as reduce inflammation and improve overall urinary function. These supplements may help alleviate discomfort and improve quality of life for individuals experiencing these conditions.
